title: Quick check: Server vs client state

questions.prompt: Why do client-state tools (Redux, Zustand) fail at managing server data?

choices.text: Server state has fundamentally different problems than UI state: caching across components and remounts, deduplication (two components fetching same URL → one request), stale-while-revalidate (show cached + refetch in background), refetch on focus/reconnect, optimistic updates with rollback, automatic retries, request cancellation. Client-state tools just store values; they don't manage the data-fetching lifecycle. Teams using Redux/Zustand for server data end up reimplementing these features manually — error-prone and tedious. Use TanStack Query, SWR, or RTK Query for server data; keep client-state tools for UI state (theme, modals, form drafts).

questions.explanation: Server state needs caching, dedup, stale-while-revalidate, refetch — client-state tools just store values.

questions.prompt: After mutating a post via useMutation, the post list still shows old data. Fix?

choices.text: Invalidate the query in `onSuccess`: `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'] })`. TanStack Query marks all queries with that key as stale and refetches them — the post list automatically updates with the new data. The query key system is how the library knows what to invalidate; hierarchical keys let one invalidation refresh many related queries. For instant feedback, pair with optimistic updates via `onMutate`: update the cache before the mutation fires, rollback in `onError` if it fails. The most common server-state bug is forgetting to invalidate after mutations.

questions.explanation: Invalidate the query key after mutation. queryClient.invalidateQueries triggers refetch.
